rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scopes for implementation-level signal routing bindings.
Use implementation scopes for assignments and bindings that are valid in that scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annexes only in appropriate component or implementation contexts.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE annex content.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation-level bindings require placement in component implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference ports or data types not explicitly defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
